title: Love Doesn't Leave
blurb: A memorial tribute about the way love stays — in the kitchen, in the garden, in every song they used to hum.

title: She Left Us a Party (A Song for Lenay)
blurb: Lenay wanted joy instead of tears, so her family sent us her story and we sent back a celebration.

title: The Moment I Knew (He Got Down on One Knee)
blurb: Written in secret for a proposal — she said yes before the last chorus ended.

title: Forever and a Day
blurb: A memorial song about love that never leaves, written for anyone who ever promised forever and meant it.

title: When Eddie Wayne Calls the Game in Heaven
blurb: A farewell to Luray's Voice of the Bulldogs, still calling the plays from the best seat in the house.

title: God Kept His Hand On Me
blurb: A Vietnam veteran's love story, carried home through fifty years of grace.

title: Worth Every Mile
blurb: A dark-country military tribute for the ones who serve far from home — and the ones who wait for them.

title: We Love You, Mrs. Gaynor
blurb: Lincoln High's thank-you to a teacher who spent a whole career believing in kids.

title: Another Candle, Still You (For Rachel)
blurb: A heartfelt birthday song for Rachel, because some people just get better with every candle.

title: Turn It Up, Jess (It's Your Night)
blurb: A feel-good birthday anthem made to be played loud on Jess's big night.

title: Two Hundred Fifty Years
blurb: A Fourth of July anthem for America's 250th, and for every small town that still lines Main Street for the parade.

title: First Man
blurb: A Father's Day song for every daughter whose first hero taught her what love should look like.

title: M-O-T-H-E-R
blurb: A Mother's Day country ballad that spells out everything she means, one letter at a time.

title: Every Day I Do
blurb: A Valentine's love song — the finale of our Stories of Love series — about choosing each other every single morning.
